strategy: Buy $315 Call — Aug 2026 (enter post May 11 earnings)

wheelStrategy.ineligibleReason: Stock price $307.81 — 1 CSP contract requires ~$30,781 cash. Exceeds $10,000 per-contract threshold for a standard retail portfolio. Use Calls/Spreads strategy only.
wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering premium-selling positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: N/A — Wheel not applicable for this stock

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter after May 11 Q1 earnings IV crush settles. Stock at $303.63 — $11.37 below $315 strike. Wait for post-earnings consolidation, then enter on any dip toward $298–302.
exitRules.profitExit: Close at 60–80% gain on premium. First target: stock at $345 (+14%) for ~80–100% gain. Second target: $380 (+25%) for ~200% gain.
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not cleared $325+ by ~June 20 (45% of time elapsed on Sep position), close. Theta accelerates sharply in the 30 days before the July 25 close.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40–50% loss on premium paid. High-IV nuclear name can gap — protect capital.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close by July 25 (5 days before July 30 Q2 earnings) — no exceptions. Nuclear stocks move sharply on EPS guidance updates.

options.bestFor: Too short — only 21 days remain at the mandatory July 25 close. Paying for 96 days of time but forced to exit before 75 have elapsed.
Sweet spot — 56 days of time value at the July 25 exit. Post-May-earnings entry gives a clean pre-earnings window to Q2. IV at 55% is manageable for a single-leg call.
Excessive time premium for a catalyst-driven name. Two earnings cycles with nuclear regulatory headline risk. Sep 19 provides better risk/reward.

recommendation.title: Single-Leg Call — Slight OTM, Enter Post-Earnings
recommendation.entryNote: ⚠️ Q1 earnings May 11 — enter AFTER to avoid IV crush. Stock is 34% off its October 2025 peak ($412). Nuclear power premium story (AI data center demand) remains intact despite regulatory headwinds. EPS guidance $11–12 for 2026.

companyOverview: Largest US electricity producer post-Calpine acquisition. Operates 21 nuclear plants providing carbon-free baseload power — the preferred energy source for AI data center co-location agreements. $1B DOE loan for Crane Clean Energy Center restart. Long-term power agreements tied to AI data center buildout. Stock down 34% from $412 peak on regulatory headwinds from FERC tightening behind-the-meter rules.

insiderFlow.summary: Minimal recent activity — standard utility executive RSU vesting sales. No open-market buying. Neutral signal for a large-cap utility.

transactions.note: Standard RSU vesting tax withholding by executives. No discretionary open-market buying or selling flagged. Utility executives rarely make open-market trades — RSU programs dominate.

insiderFlow.signal: NEUTRAL — no notable Form 4 activity. Utility companies have low insider ownership and minimal open-market trading. No bullish or bearish signal from insiders.

catalysts: Largest US nuclear operator — premium positioning for AI data center power deals
$1B DOE loan guarantee for Crane Clean Energy Center restart (new nuclear capacity)
Stock 34% off peak — asymmetric risk/reward vs. underlying earnings growth
AI infrastructure buildout requiring reliable carbon-free baseload power
risks: ⚠️ Three Mile Island reconnection DELAYED TO 2031 — PJM grid operator confirmed TMI cannot connect to grid until 2031, four years later than the originally planned 2027. This removes a key near-term revenue catalyst.
FERC tightening behind-the-meter co-location rules — core revenue model under regulatory threat
Grid interconnection delays already impacting 2026 guidance; TMI delay is the most concrete example
Aging nuclear fleet: decommissioning costs, compliance complexity
Scotiabank cut target from $481 to $441; Morgan Stanley cut from $385 to $360
Q1 EPS guidance shortfall ($11–12 vs. analyst expectations of $11.60+)
Earnings May 11 — IV crush risk if guidance disappoints again; TMI delay news may already be partially priced in
dilution: Standard utility capital structure. $1B DOE loan is debt-financed, not dilutive. No significant equity dilution risk.
